title: [CMake] Local install doesn't produce CIRCT targets in lib/cmake/mlir/MLIRTargets.cmake
body:
# Context

Platform: macOS 14.1.1
Architecture: arm64
circt commit: Reproduced on both 4f1b8b26b and firtool-1.59.0

# Issue Description

I'm trying to test out the [firtool-standalone-plugin repo](https://github.com/uenoku/firtool-standalone-plugin) locally (platform is macOS arm64), trying to exercise the functionality added in https://github.com/llvm/circt/pull/6254. I can't use the released `circt-full-shared-macos-x64` package, since I get errors linking x86-64 libraries during an arm64 build. Since there's no macOS-arm64 release build, I'm doing a local build/install, but when I build `firtool-standalone-plugin` with that local build, I get this CMake error:

```
CMake Error at /path/to/local/circt/install/lib/cmake/circt/CIRCTConfig.cmake:10 (find_package):
  Found package configuration file:

    /path/to/local/firtool-standalone-plugin/../circt/install/lib/cmake/mlir/MLIRConfig.cmake

  but it set MLIR_FOUND to FALSE so package "MLIR" is considered to be NOT
  FOUND.  Reason given by package:

  The following imported targets are referenced, but are missing:
  CIRCTAffineToLoopSchedule CIRCTArcToLLVM CIRCTCalyxToFSM CIRCTCalyxToHW
  CIRCTCalyxNative CIRCTCombToArith CIRCTCombToLLVM CIRCTConvertToArcs
  CIRCTDCToHW CIRCTExportChiselInterface CIRCTExportVerilog CIRCTFIRRTLToHW
  CIRCTFSMToSV CIRCTHandshakeToDC CIRCTHandshakeToHW CIRCTHWArithToHW
  CIRCTHWToLLHD CIRCTHWToLLVM CIRCTHWToSV CIRCTHWToSystemC CIRCTLLHDToLLVM
  CIRCTLoopScheduleToCalyx CIRCTMooreToCore CIRCTPipelineToHW CIRCTSCFToCalyx
  CIRCTSeqToSV CIRCTCFToHandshake CIRCTVerifToSV CIRCTExportFIRRTL CIRCTComb
  CIRCTESI CIRCTFIRRTL CIRCTMSFT CIRCTMSFTTransforms CIRCTHW CIRCTLLHD
  CIRCTMoore CIRCTOM CIRCTOMEvaluator CIRCTSeq CIRCTSeqTransforms CIRCTSV
  CIRCTSVTransforms CIRCTFSM CIRCTFSMTransforms CIRCTHandshake
  CIRCTHandshakeTransforms CIRCTHWArith CIRCTVerif CIRCTLTL CIRCTFirtool

Call Stack (most recent call first):
  CMakeLists.txt:12 (find_package)
```

And indeed, if I diff my locally-built `lib/cmake/mlir/MLIRTargets.cmake` file with the one in the `firtool-1.59.0` `circt-full-shared-*` release, I see that many of the CIRCT targets are missing from my locally-built copy, such as this one:

```
# Create imported target CIRCTFirtool
add_library(CIRCTFirtool SHARED IMPORTED)

set_target_properties(CIRCTFirtool PROPERTIES
  INTERFACE_LINK_LIBRARIES "..."
)
```

It doesn't seem like there's a clearly-documented flow for reproducing the released artifacts (the two CMake commands in the README are quite different from the command run during the CI-release action), so I extracted the following CMake command from the `.github/workflows` area:

```
CIRCT_DIR="$(pwd)"
CIRCT_INSTALL_DIR="$CIRCT_DIR/install"
BUILD_TYPE=Release
ASSERT=ON
COMPILE_COMMANDS=ON
SHARED=ON
STATS=ON
CLANG_DIR=/opt/homebrew/opt/llvm/bin
C_COMPILER="$CLANG_DIR/clang"
CXX_COMPILER="$CLANG_DIR/clang++"

mkdir build
cd build

cmake \
    -G Ninja \
    -S "$CIRCT_DIR/llvm/llvm" \
    -DCMAKE_BUILD_TYPE=$BUILD_TYPE \
    -DCMAKE_C_COMPILER=$C_COMPILER \
    -DCMAKE_CXX_COMPILER=$CXX_COMPILER \
    -DCMAKE_INSTALL_PREFIX="$CIRCT_INSTALL_DIR" \
    -DCMAKE_EXPORT_COMPILE_COMMANDS=$COMPILE_COMMANDS \
    -DBUILD_SHARED_LIBS=$SHARED \
    -DLLVM_BUILD_TOOLS=ON \
    -DLLVM_BUILD_EXAMPLES=OFF \
    -DLLVM_ENABLE_ASSERTIONS=$ASSERT \
    -DLLVM_ENABLE_PROJECTS=mlir \
    -DLLVM_EXTERNAL_PROJECTS=circt \
    -DLLVM_EXTERNAL_CIRCT_SOURCE_DIR=".." \
    -DLLVM_STATIC_LINK_CXX_STDLIB=ON \
    -DLLVM_ENABLE_TERMINFO=OFF \
    -DLLVM_PARALLEL_LINK_JOBS=1 \
    -DLLVM_TARGETS_TO_BUILD="host" \
    -DLLVM_FORCE_ENABLE_STATS=$STATS \
    -DLLVM_ENABLE_ZSTD=OFF \
    -DVERILATOR_DISABLE=ON \
    -DLLVM_INSTALL_UTILS=ON

ninja check-circt check-circt-unit
ninja install
```

Any clues as to why this might be happening? Happy to provide any extra info that might help.
